text: So I'm presenting a sixty-seven-year-old female, former housewife, with the following known diseases. Arterial hypertension, diabetes, she performed a thyroidectomy in the past, and also a carpal syndrome surgery.
She is currently medicated with glicazide, metformin, levothyroxine, olmezartan, simvastatin, and ethyl loflacepate.
In January of this current year, she had her first episode of macroscopic hematuria.
She then performed a cystoscopy that showed a four to five centimeter right wall bladder tumor, and she went through in April of this year to a TURB that fully resected the tumor.
In May, her histology was positive for a muscle invasive bladder tumor with no positive lymph nodes and no metastasis.
The left image displays a thickened bladder wall near the retral meatus, while the right image shows thinning of the renal parenchyma along with retrohydronephrosis.
Dr. Peter Viklund will be performing a robotic radical cystectomy, followed by an extended lymphonectomy, and afterwards, Dr. Inderberg-Gill will perform intercorporeal orthotropic neobladder.
Okay, so welcome. I don't know if, do we still have the external camera? So welcome to the OR-One today, where we're going to do a cystectomy.

We started a little bit, I will show you in a minute, but I first like Rodolfo Sanchez here, who is accompanying me from Karolinska, is assisting today. And I have a wonderful crew, also local here, that are helping me. And hopefully we can understand each other also. We speak Spanish. English, Portuguese and Swedish in here. So we will hope that we get everything right in the end.

Now we will see maybe it's in the gill who will do the stapling in the end. So we will see. But that's how everything is set up here.

I always do this operation as a very standardized procedure, try to do all cystectomies exactly the same way. So we always start dissecting the ureter on the right side. And you start on the right side for one reason, and that is because normally the sigmoid is attached to the left side, so you almost have a grasper pulling the intestine over to the left side when you start. So we take advantage of that, basically.
